title: Spat my coffee after seeing Jaffe listed as Guest of Honor
display_title: Spat my coffee after seeing Jaffe listed as Guest of Honor
wikitext:
[]: Spat my coffee after seeing Jaffe listed as Guest of Honor..             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Jun 11, 2011 2:18 am Anime Expo 2011 Announces Voice Actors Toshiyuki Morikawa and Taliesin Jaffe as Guests of Honor Ah, WTF. A term, popularized by Dr. Nick, that refers to the notorious commentary track spanning Manga Entertainment's two NGE releases (Death & Rebirth and End of Evangelion), featuring the ever-insightful Amanda Winn Lee, Jason C. Lee (her husband and dub voice of Aoba), and Taliesin Jaffe (who does… something or other). While the track has some comedic merit, it is good for little else, packed head-to-toe with misinformation and shameless endorsement of the movies' dub (which even NGE dubbies tend to agree is a disappointment!) and the oftentimes horrifying decisions she made producing it (including this one). Jaffe gets a break because he at least attempted to present some worthwhile material, even if he wasn't 100% on track either (“Samhel”?). For something meant to accompany a very complicated and serious work of art, their commentary is offensively inadequate. Adding insult to injury, of course, is the fact that many of the poor n00bs who listen to it will, not knowing any better, parrot the commentary's BS until set straight by more wisened geeks.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Sat Jun 11, 2011 2:46 am And this is spit-take worthy why?

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sat Jun 11, 2011 3:01 am ^Good question....

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Jun 11, 2011 3:46 am On second thought... It'll be interesting to see an encounter between Mr. Snausages (if he could make it ) and that honored guest.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sat Jun 11, 2011 6:06 am I think the other guests including J-POP group Kalafina and Chinese/Japanese blogger Danny Choo are way more interesting guests. I mean its Kalafina! If you've heard their singles lately, they're good!

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Jun 11, 2011 7:08 am Danny Choo? Seriously?

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Sat Jun 11, 2011 7:09 am *has no idea who those names are*

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Jun 11, 2011 11:20 am For those that dont know, Jaffe is on the EoE commentary and appears to often be hailed (mostly by himself) as some sort of Eva expert. Thats 'Expert' as in 'can waffle pretentiously about religeous imagery and how its all about sex'.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Jun 11, 2011 12:56 pm Thanks, I forgot to mention about doing a backgrounder on Jaffe. For further info explaining why, read here: http://wiki.evageeks.org/The_Geektionary#Commentary_of_Evil Electric Sachiel wrote: I think the other guests including J-POP group Kalafina and Chinese/Japanese blogger Danny Choo are way more interesting guests. I mean its Kalafina! If you've heard their singles lately, they're good!

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sat Jun 11, 2011 1:07 pm soul.assassin wrote: Offtopic: PMMM introduced me to Kalafina. :)

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Jun 11, 2011 1:42 pm Oh, you guys. Taliesin Jaffe is a pretty cool dude. If any Hellsing fans are out there, he's friends with Kohta Hirano and is basically in charge of all the Hellsing dubs. He's also worked on dubbing Beck , Read Or Die and Street Fighter IV . I'm sure he's already had plenty of fanboys whine to him about the commentary he recorded 10 years ago.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jun 11, 2011 2:13 pm Sephiroth's seiyuu, cool~! I only attended A/X once, it was A/X 04, but still a blast. I also went to Fanime 2009 and Fanime 2010... but anyway. I sense nerd-rage upcoming from some in the fandom. From what little I recall reading and hearing of the Commentary, Jaffe at least had decent observations to make, even if he was off the mark in his thoughts... At least it wasn't the complete B.S. of AWL or her husband! (I mean, "There's no word for "I" in Japanese"? Seriously?)

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Sat Jun 11, 2011 2:24 pm The guy plugged an Alan Moore comic that isn't any of the DC works. He has my approval, just for that. Lame reason, isn't it? xD

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Jun 11, 2011 7:05 pm Well the good hes done generally outweighs the portions of wibbling in EoE commentary but I still reserve my right to refer to him as Tasmin Jaffa-Cake
